substep.title: Assemble the Grid
substep.action: Build the matrix: alternatives as rows, criteria as columns (or the reverse when there are more criteria than alternatives — pick the orientation that reads on one screen). Each cell states the alternative's standing on that criterion in a few words, with an inline citation (file:line, URL, or thoughts/ alias). A cell with no evidence reads "could not determine", never a guess.

substep.title: Surface Per-Criterion Tradeoffs
substep.action: Below the grid, for each criterion, state which alternative(s) lead and on what evidence — factually, one line per criterion ("Performance: A leads on throughput benchmarks [cite]; B leads on tail latency [cite]"). This is where the tradeoff structure becomes legible without collapsing it into a score.

substep.title: Compose the Document Body
substep.action:
Compose the body per references/research/document-format body-skeleton. Fixed top: the comparison question and a Summary stating the SHAPE of the tradeoff ("A optimizes for X at the cost of Y; B is the inverse; C trails both but is simplest to adopt") — never a winner or a weighted total. Variable middle: the matrix grid, then the per-criterion tradeoff notes, then a short per-alternative narrative. Bottom: Open Questions (criteria left undetermined, alternatives deferred at the web gate).
The decision is the reader's; the matrix equips it.

check.template-output:
Present the matrix for review before persistence.
[a] Revise (re-research a cell, add a criterion) [c] Continue to persistence [y] YOLO
